channel.title: Maine Judicial Branch News & Announcements
channel.description: General Judicial Branch newsroom — a date-prefixed list of announcement links (new courthouses, eCourts rollout, bar-exam and administrative news), broader than the eFiling-specific jb-efiling-notices and the closure-focused jb-court-alerts. Same 'ul.newslist li' structure as jb-efiling-notices.

item.title: 11/2/2023: SJC invites comments on proposed amendments to Rule 80D of the Maine Rules of Civil Procedure regarding forcible entry and detainer actions. Comments due by November 16, 2023.
11/17/2023: Thanksgiving Holiday Closure Notice -- 2:00 p.m. on Nov. 22-24, 2023
12/1/2023: SJC seeks member of public to serve on Guardian Ad Litem Review Board. Applications due by December 29, 2023.
12/12/2023: SJC invites comments on proposed amendments to Rules 126 and 127 of the Maine Rules of Civil Procedure regarding probate matters in the District Court. Comments due by December 26, 2023.
12/18/2023: Holiday Closures
1/4/2024: Maine Judicial Branch Responds to Bomb Threat
1/16/2024: Judicial Branch Warns of Court-related Scams
2/8/2024: Judicial Branch Announces Expansion of Maine eCourts to Rumford, Farmington, and South Paris Courts
2/20/2024: Chief Justice Valerie Stanfill to Deliver State of the Judiciary
2/21/2024: Read Chief Justice Stanfill's 2024 State of the Judiciary Address
2/27/2024: Upcoming eFiler Virtual Training Sessions
3/11/2024: How to Become an Interpreter Information Session

3/14/2024: SJC invites comments on proposed amendments to the Maine Rules of Appellate Procedure regarding electronic filing and service of documents; motions practice; and sentence appeals. Comments due by April 11, 2024.
3/21/2024: Franklin and Oxford County Courts Prepare for April 1st Launch of Maine eCourts and eFiling
4/1/2024: Maine eCourts and eFiling Expands to Rumford, Farmington, and South Paris Courts
5/8/2024: Judicial Branch Is Adding Protection from Abuse and Harassment Cases in Courts that have Implemented eFiling
5/9/2024: TEMPORARY CLOSURE NOTICE: NO PUBLIC PARKING IN THE CUMBERLAND COUNTY PARKING GARAGE
5/16/2024: Morton A. Brody Distinguished Judicial Service Award 2024

5/17/2024: SJC invites comments on proposed amendments to the Maine Rules of Appellate Procedure regarding motions for reconsideration and page numbering in briefs and appendices. Comments due by June 14, 2024.
5/17/2024: SJC invites comments on a proposed amendment to the Maine Bar Admission Rules regarding applicant interviews. Comments due by June 14, 2024
5/24/2024: Judicial Branch Courts will be closed on Monday, May 27, 2024 to commemorate Memorial Day.
5/30/2024: Applications Open for the 2024 Access to Justice Tax Credit Program
6/10/2024: Judicial Branch Adds Protection from Abuse and Harassment Cases to eFileMaine
6/14/2024: Judicial Branch Statement Regarding Not Building Ellsworth Courthouse on Current Site
6/17/2024: Judicial Branch courts will be closed Wed., June 19, 2024
6/17/2024: Judicial Branch Statement Regarding Leein Hinkley
7/1/2024: Judicial Branch courts will be closed July 4th & 5th
7/8/2024: Upcoming Court Interpreter Orientation
7/9/2024: Judicial Branch Recognizes Employees for Outstanding Work
8/1/2024: Maine Justice Foundation Announces Recipients of the 2024 Access to Justice Tax Credit Program

8/2/2024: SJC invites comments on proposed amendment to the Maine Bar Admission Rules regarding temporary permission to practice with the Maine Commission on Public Defense Services. Comments due by August 23, 2024.

8/2/2024: SJC invites comments on proposed amendment to the Maine Code of Judicial Conduct regarding application of the Code to candidates for nomination to a judicial office. Comments due by August 23, 2024.

8/7/2024: Updated Aug. 12, 2024: SJC invites comments on proposed amendments to the Maine Rules of Small Claims Procedure regarding service of the statement of claim and notice of disclosure hearing. Comments due by September 4, 2024.
8/28/2024: SJC invites comments on a proposed amendment to the Maine Bar Rules that would provide CLE credit for pro bono service. Comments due by September 25, 2024.
8/30/2024: Judicial Branch Courts Closed Monday, September 2, 2024
10/1/2024: Supreme Judicial Court to Hold Oral Arguments at Three Maine High Schools
10/10/2024: Judicial Branch courts will be closed Monday, October 14, 2024
11/8/2024: Judicial Branch Courts to be Closed on Monday, November 11
11/27/2024: Judicial Branch Thanksgiving Schedule
12/10/2024: Judicial Branch Announces Expansion of eFiling to include Criminal and Juvenile Cases in Lewiston and Auburn Courts
12/24/2024: Judicial Branch Holiday Schedule
12/31/2024: Law Court invites amicus briefs on appointment and authority of municipal park rangers. Amicus briefs due by January 27, 2025
1/9/2025: SJC invites comments on proposed amendments to rules governing the Committee on Judicial Conduct and the consideration of reports of the Committee. Comments due by January 23, 2025.
1/10/2025: Law Court invites amicus briefs regarding the effect of a party's death during an appeal from a divorce judgment. Amicus briefs due by February 10, 2025
1/17/2025: Courts and the Administrative Office of the Courts Will Be Closed on January 20th
1/17/2025: SJC invites comments on proposed amendment to the Maine Rules of Evidence to add an exception to the rule against hearsay. Comments due by February 14, 2025.
1/27/2025: Newport District Court Closed Until Further Notice; In-Person Proceedings to be held in Bangor

1/28/2025: SJC invites comments on proposed amendments to the Maine Rules of Probate Procedure regarding electronic filing and public access to electronic court records. Comments due by February 25, 2025.
1/28/2025: Building housing the Newport District Court closed until further notice
7/13/2026: Aroostook County Adult Treatment & Recovery Court Accepts First Participant
